location: A 2% chance drop from Greatsword-wielding, lion-headed Imps. The two confirmed spawns are inside Fog Rift Catacombs (the very first Imp encountered there, reachable via a short northwest path from the Castle Front Site of Grace and down a lift, is the commonly farmed one) and inside Scorpion River Catacombs. Note: a few secondary guides (Game8, GameRant) also claim a guaranteed pickup near a staircase in Darklight Catacombs, but this could not be confirmed on either wiki.gg or Fextralife, whose item lists for that dungeon do not include it — treat that claim with caution.
tactics: Fog Rift Catacombs is reached from the Castle Front Site of Grace by heading northwest past a small enemy encampment and along a trail of gravestones to two lit braziers marking the entrance.

Kill the first Lion Imp inside for a shot at the drop, then rest at the Fog Rift Catacombs interior grace (down the entrance lift) to reset and repeat; gear that boosts Item Discovery (Silver Tear Mask, Silver-Pickled Fowl Foot, Gold Scarab talisman) speeds up the farm considerably.
The same enemy type also spawns in Scorpion River Catacombs, in the Ancient Ruins of Rauh region far to the north, if you'd rather farm there instead — it has its own grace just inside the entrance.
Only the lion-headed Imps actually carrying a greatsword drop this weapon — don't waste time farming the smaller unarmed or dagger-wielding Imp variants found throughout the same dungeons.
lore: An oddity favored by Imps: a greatsword smithed into the shape of a fork-tongued lizard. Its strong attacks let the wielder fire the lizard's forked tongue out as a projectile alongside the swing.

builds.name: Dex/Str Blood hybrid
builds.why: Base scaling leans Dexterity over Strength and the weapon is fully infusable, so a Blood affinity lets a Dex-leaning build stack bleed procs from both the melee swing and its ranged tongue projectile.

builds.name: Keen Dex zoner
builds.why: Keen affinity pushes Dexterity scaling especially high, giving the best overall damage of any greatsword build while its unique heavy-attack projectile offers poke/zoning most greatswords lack.
